mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of months where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate, specifically a rise of 0.5 percentage points or more over three months, signals a deteriorating labor market and economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 30% of instances following a triggered UNRATE increase, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is rising while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 1 month AND CPIAUCSL > 3.0%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS is rising and CPIAUCSL is above 3.0%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, a sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 exhibits negative 5-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances during confirmed economic expansion periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO), specifically a drop of 1.0% or more month-over-month, signals weakening manufacturing and overall supply-side constraints, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 35% of instances following a triggered INDPRO decline, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of months where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds 4.0%, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) growth is strong (above 2% annualized) and retail sales (RSAFS) are also growing robustly (above 0.5% month-over-month), it signifies broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 30% of instances when both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth conditions are met, the hypothesis is rejected.
